## [18:11 - 21:10]

It's a general store and you know for people who run general stores and wonder why things are not selling This is why because you're not selling anything beyond a general product and for people who just overload their pages and put all the latest Shopify widgets and just you know want to do all these crazy things and wonder why I think people are not buying Probably because you're not allowing them to buy you're actually put making it more difficult You're actually putting blockers up that are stopping people buying And it's all it's all about focusing on what's right for your brand and product and doing the testing and getting that right as well So, you know the key thing about a good product pages don't over complicate it Keep it and it keep it simple and keep you know focus on the key thing like what's the number one thing you want people to do on this page? Add to cart so make that the clearest call to action What do people want like don't just focus on what you want? What do people want? They want to see the picture they want to see multiple angles. They want to know the name of the product They want to know they're on the right Page for example, okay here. There aren't any more sizes to choose, but they want to know for example, you know What does medium mean? What does that look like for me? They want to have a bit of Explanation leaks on the actual watch itself if you've got anything like this like I would probably highlight this better Maybe give it a kind of black background with white copy or whatever it is Just maybe not because of the button but give that a bit more clarity because free shipping free returns 24 months warranty They're all strong selling points as well But they're selling an experience and this I would probably have put a few more lifestyle images Maybe even pulled out from their Instagram page as well and actually make that a bit more of a kind of Deeper experience to show what other people are how other people are a kind of experience in their watch as well I mean these guys have a ton of content on On Instagram again, they could kind of embellish their page with some Instagram pictures as well and really for these guys I Would really really kind of cut down on a lot of the stuff here So for example if I was to come in and take this page on and this is another thing for you I would a be tested I would go through this page completely rip it apart and fix it back together and literally a B split test it I'd recommend visual website optimizer There's Google website optimizer as well and a few other tools out there But you can literally take your Facebook traffic and split it into Doesn't matter if you're doing this for a client or for your own site Take that page rip it apart do what you feel is going to work best Create a page that you feel is going to actually convert that product and split test it and quite quickly You'll see the impact on conversion and you'll be able to kind of cut through so you know That's kind of the core thing about product pages assuming that you know It whether using this is a landing page or you know, ultimately people are going to have to go through this for your e-commerce store Make sure it's as clean as possible
